pattern: Only interior runs of three or more same-colored pieces ever let a player move, so the winner is decided purely by counting how many 'A' triples and 'B' triples exist along the line.
hints: Focus on runs of identical letters; a lone piece or a piece next to a differently-colored neighbor can never be removed by anyone.
Inside a maximal run of the same letter, walk it and count every interior position whose left and right neighbor both match it -- that count is exactly how many times that run can be played.
Sum those counts separately for 'A' and for 'B' across the whole string; Alice needs a strictly larger total than Bob, so return countA > countB.

edge_inputs.description: single piece, no possible moves for either player
two same letters, both on the edge, no legal moves
one legal Alice move inside a short run of A
minimum length string of a single letter
one long uninterrupted run so only one color ever gets to move
strict alternation leaves no interior triple for anyone
Bob's run is longer than Alice's, so he ends up with more moves
equal-length runs of each color tie on move count, so Bob still wins

stress.description: uniform random A/B string up to the real length limit
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('AB') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]

stress.description: built from randomly sized runs so long same-color stretches are common, exercising the run-counting logic harder than pure noise
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    parts = []
    total = 0
    while total < n:
        run_len = min(rng.randint(1, 6), n - total)
        ch = rng.choice('AB')
        parts.append(ch * run_len)
        total += run_len
    return [''.join(parts)]
